title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. sphagnicola
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. sphagnicola, also known as the marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce blood pressure. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. sphagnicola include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. sphagnicola can be made by infusing the plant's roots in water or using it in combination with other herbs in teas, tinctures, and salves.
